title: anthemis cretica subsp. leucanthemoides
intro: Anthemis cretica subsp. leucanthemoides, also known as mountain chamomile or white daisy, is a subspecies of Anthemis cretica, a flowering plant in the daisy family. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness due to its sedative and calming effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and essential oils, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Herbal preparations of Anthemis cretica subsp. leucanthemoides, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
